raw_consumer_statement:
An American Express cardholder booked a short-term lodging reservation through the XXXX platform and paid {$1000.00} using an American Express card . The booking was governed by a clearly disclosed non-refundable cancellation policy presented at the time of booking. The cardholder also signed a rental agreement prior to arrival reaffirming the same non-refundable terms.

On the scheduled day of check-in, a temporary electrical safety issue affected interior lighting at the originally booked property. For safety and habitability reasons, the merchant promptly tendered performance by offering a comparable alternate accommodation for the same dates with sufficient sleeping capacity. The cardholder declined the alternate accommodation solely due to personal location preference and requested cancellation.

Despite documented evidence showing the disclosed non-refundable terms, the signed agreement, proof of tendered performance, and the cardholders written refusal and request for cancellation, American Express decided the dispute in the cardholders favor and reversed the transaction. The decision did not include an explanation of how the contractual terms or tendered performance were evaluated.
